exemplar.title: Study of the effect of running session times (6:00, 10:00 , 12:00 , 16:00, 20:00) on heart rate response in a male participant aged 18 during 5 minute exercise intervals

detailedExemplarData.title: Study of the effect of running session times (6:00, 10:00 , 12:00 , 16:00, 20:00) on heart rate response in a male participant aged 18 during 5 minute exercise intervals

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question set in a precise circadian physiology context
Methodological considerations are well justified for sampling times and controls
Procedure description is clear enough to allow reproduction
Introduction synthesises peerâreviewed sources to justify the design
weaknesses: Hypothesis section is incomplete and contains extraneous punctuation
Controlled variables table omits some methods and explanations
Dependent variable description cuts off before the calculation formula
Treadmill speed and gradient are not specified
Safety table lacks clear column separation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Research Design fall at the top of band 5, showing a highly focused question, fully explained methodological reasoning and a reproducible procedure. Minor omissions (hypothesis truncation, missing treadmill details, table formatting) prevent award of the full 6 marks. A score of 4 reflects strong performance across all strands with scope for polishing these details.

strengths: Raw and processed data are presented with most headings, units and uncertainties, making the presentation clear
Sample calculation and SD formula are transparent and aid comprehension
Appropriate use of ANOVA and t-test demonstrates relevant data processing
weaknesses: Uncertainty values are inconsistently populated and propagation methods are sometimes inappropriate
Graph lacks error bars for all points
ANOVA table contains placeholders rather than full F and p statistics
T-test type and assumptions are not specified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data Analysis shows clear communication and largely appropriate processing (band 3â4), but inconsistent uncertainty handling, missing statistical details and graphical omissions prevent a topâband score. A 3 reflects solid analytical work with some significant omissions to address.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question with numerical trends
Findings are fully consistent with ANOVA and t-test results
Invokes circadian biology to contextualise observed effects
weaknesses: Comparison to literature is descriptive without quantitative alignment
No critical evaluation of discrepancies between results and published norms
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is clearly justified and consistent with the analysis (band 5â6 for consistency) and makes some relevant scientific comparisons (band 3â4). However, lack of quantitative integration with literature prevents the highest band for scientific context. A score of 3 balances these strengths and limitations.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses linked to validity
Proposes realistic improvements directly addressing identified limitations
weaknesses: Does not analyse the relative impact of each limitation
âStrengths of the investigationâ section is empty
Improvement explanations lack depth and quantitative justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation describes specific weaknesses and relevant improvements (band 3â4), but stops at description without explaining their impact or how enhancements improve validity. A score of 2 reflects a basic but incomplete evaluation requiring deeper analysis.

strandSelections.reasoning: The stated conclusion directly answers the research question, cites the numerical trend (30.2 bpm at 6:00 vs 17-19 bpm at 20:00) and links this to statistically significant ANOVA and t-test outcomes, demonstrating full consistency with the analysis.  Justification is explicit and unambiguous, warranting placement in the 5-6 band.

Raw data (Appendix D) and processed means/SD values are presented in tables and a graph; most headings, units and uncertainties are shown, so the communication is clear.  Precision is reduced by distorted table layouts and some stray symbols, meaning the presentation is not simultaneously impeccably clear and precise.  Hence the strand sits best in the 3-4 band.

Data processing includes calculation of individual changes, means, standard deviations, an ANOVA across five groups, and a targeted t-test between extreme times.  Although minor transcription errors appear in a few table cells, the calculations and statistical choices are largely appropriate to the research question and support the conclusion.  Small inconsistencies prevent award of the top band, but the work clearly exceeds the minimum accuracy threshold for 3-4.

The evaluation table lists four concrete methodological weaknesses (small sample, uncontrolled environment, male-only focus, self-report bias) and briefly explains each.  However, their relative impact on data quality is not analysed; the discussion stops at description.  This aligns with the 3-4 band descriptor of âspecific methodological weaknesses are described.â

The student invokes circadian biology (core temperature, cortisol rhythms) to contextualise why morning sessions evoke higher cardiovascular strain.  While references are cited, the comparison remains descriptive rather than analyticalâthere is no quantitative alignment with literature values or critical evaluation of discrepancies.  Thus the strand meets âsome relevant comparisonâ but not the depth implied by âjustified through relevant comparison,â meriting the 3-4 band.

The student formulates a focused question that specifies both the population (18-year-old male) and the treatment levels (five clearly stated clock times).  Two introductory sections synthesise peer-reviewed sources on circadian physiology and explicitly relate these ideas to expected heart-rate responses, thereby embedding the question in a precise biological context rather than a generic exercise framework.  Because the context is narrow (age, sex, exercise mode) and biologically justified, the work comfortably meets the descriptor of being âdescribed within a specific and appropriate context.â

Instrument uncertainties (Â±1 bpm, Â±0.01 s, Â±10 mL) are stated and propagated qualitatively into combined uncertainties for mean change values.  However, propagation is inconsistent (some rows lack an uncertainty figure) and the method for âuncertainty = (maxâmin)/2â is not always appropriate for means.  Significant treatment of uncertainty is evident but marred by omissions and methodological weaknesses, fitting the mid-band descriptor.

Four realistic improvements are stated and directly linked to the weaknesses (e.g., enlarge sample, climate-controlled venue, demographic diversity, objective compliance monitoring).  The student does not elaborate on how each improvement would quantitatively enhance reliability or validity, so the explanation level remains descriptive rather than explanatory, placing the work in the 3-4 band.

Multiple strands of methodological thinking are explained: the need for five evenly spaced time points to map circadian variation, the choice of 5-minute bouts after a pilot that demonstrated greater discriminatory power, and the inclusion of five repeats per slot to yield sufficient data for ANOVA.  Key controlled variables (hydration volume, pre-exercise abstention, treadmill speed, participant characteristics) are justified biologically.  These explanations show clear awareness of what data are required to answer the question and why the selected protocol should provide them, matching the highest-level descriptor.

The step-by-step procedure includes session scheduling, rest-period timing, heart-rate monitor calibration, exact volumes and uncertainties for water, and repeat numbers for each participant.  Although table formatting is occasionally untidy, a competent reader could reproduce the experiment without seeking clarification.  No substantive omissions (e.g., treadmill speed) hinder replication; therefore the description satisfies the âallows for the investigation to be reproducedâ requirement.

annotations.comment: The figure on exercise timing lacks a descriptive caption and axis labels. Adding a clear title, axis units, and legend will improve reader comprehension.
The research question is precisely stated with specific participant characteristics and time points, fulfilling the criterion for a focused, context-rich question.
The hypothesis section ends mid-sentence and contains extraneous punctuation. Revising to complete the rationale will enhance clarity and coherence.
The controlled variables table omits method details for some entries and truncates control explanations. Expanding and completing the table will support reproducibility.
The dependent variable description cuts off before the calculation formula. Ensure the full measurement procedure and calculation steps are clearly presented.

The introduction effectively embeds the research question within a detailed circadian physiology context, demonstrating a strong understanding of the biological mechanisms that influence heart rate variability.
The safety table entries run together and lack clear separation between concerns, problems, and mitigations. Reformatting into distinct columns will enhance clarity.
The sample calculation provides a transparent example of data processing, aiding reader comprehension of change calculations.
Qualitative observations are presented without reference to circadian timing. Linking these findings back to specific session times would strengthen their relevance.
The uncertainty column in the combined mean change table is only populated for 6:00. Providing uncertainty values for all time points will ensure consistent uncertainty consideration.
The standard deviation formula is clearly presented with definitions for each term, demonstrating precise communication of data processing.
Graph 1 lacks error bars for several data points. Including error bars across all sessions will more accurately reflect measurement uncertainty.
The ANOVA table contains placeholders (tilde symbols) instead of within-group F-statistics and p-values. Supplying complete statistical results is essential for valid interpretation.
The T-test description omits whether a paired or unpaired test was used. Specifying the test type and assumptions will clarify the appropriateness of the analysis.
The methodology section is well organized and clearly labeled, facilitating understanding of the experimental design and procedure.
Treadmill speed and gradient are not specified in the materials or procedure. Providing these details is essential for protocol reproducibility.
The pilot trial description provides a clear rationale for extending session duration, demonstrating thoughtful methodological refinement.
Nested list symbols appear as â\circâ markers due to formatting errors. Correcting bullet symbols and indentation will improve readability.
The safety and ethical considerations section clearly identifies key issues, showing awareness of participant welfare.
Pre-exercise setup omits specification of treadmill speed and incline. Including these parameters in the procedure will ensure full reproducibility.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes key findings and directly addresses the research question, fully consistent with the analysis.
The comparison to existing literature is descriptive and lacks quantitative alignment. Critically evaluating how experimental values differ from published norms would deepen the scientific context.
The âStrengths of the investigationâ table is empty. Populating this section with specific methodological strengths will complete the evaluation.
The limitations table effectively identifies specific methodological weaknesses, demonstrating thoughtful evaluation.
The suggested improvements table is incomplete and needs full explanations for each recommendation to address identified limitations effectively.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
